explanation: This command simulates a typical invocation of frpc.exe with the configuration file specified in the Sigma rule. It uses a standard command-line format and does not attempt to bypass detection.

This command uses the executable name with the .exe extension and a direct path to the configuration file. It is a straightforward representation of the process and does not attempt to bypass detection.

This command uses forward slashes instead of backslashes for the file path, which is a valid alternative in Windows command-line environments. It remains functionally equivalent and does not attempt to bypass detection.

This command uses the long-form option '-config' instead of the short-form '-c', which is a valid substitution in many command-line tools. It remains functionally equivalent and does not attempt to bypass detection.
This command uses a different directory for the configuration file while maintaining the same structure and syntax. It remains functionally equivalent and does not attempt to bypass detection.
